citations.description: test_mcp_write_fails_hard present (in-process test) but no structural guard test in this file or elsewhere

Searches for 'mcp_server.py', 'except.*Exception.*guard', 'lines.*RemovedInputError', 'test_mcp_guard', 'above.*guard', 'assert.*RemovedInputError.*raise', and re.findall/re.search patterns across all tests found no structural source-reading guard test.

findings.criterion: A pytest that reads `src/rebar/mcp_server.py` and asserts every line matching `except\s+(Exception|BaseException)` has a `RemovedInputError: raise` guard immediately above it (a reproducible check, not a hand grep) — part of AC6 for the MCP-server path test.
findings.detail: The in-process portion of AC6 (`test_mcp_write_fails_hard` in tests/interfaces/contracts/test_tombstone_registry.py) is present and tests that RemovedInputError propagates through the MCP write path. However, the structural source-reading pytest — which should read the text of src/rebar/mcp_server.py, find every `except Exception` / `except BaseException` line, and assert a `RemovedInputError: raise` guard appears immediately above each — was not found anywhere in the test suite after a comprehensive search across tests/, tests/unit/, tests/interfaces/, and tests/interfaces/contracts/.
findings.remediation: Add a pytest (e.g., in tests/unit/ or tests/interfaces/contracts/) that reads the text of src/rebar/mcp_server.py, finds every line matching the pattern `except\s+(Exception|BaseException)`, and asserts that the line immediately above each match contains `RemovedInputError: raise`.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
